entries.definition:
كتاب الحاء

حوذ - حور

(حور) الحاء والواو والراء ثلاثة أصول: أحدها لون، والآخَر الرُّجوع، والثالث أن يدور الشيء دَوْراً.

فأما الأول فالحَوَر: شدّةُ بياض العينِ في شدّةِ سوادِها. قال أبو عمرو:

241
blameworthy; an epithet of Prophet Jonah * (68:48) كَصَاحِبِ الْحُوتِ the fellow of the whale.
entries.definition_english:
241
blameworthy; an epithet of Prophet Jonah * (68:48) كَصَاحِبِ الْحُوتِ the fellow of the whale.

entries.definition:
h–w–r circle, to encircle, to return to, to go away from; to have a dialogue, entourage, disciples; discerning power; to decrease; marked contrast between black and white in a woman’s eye, fair skin; oyster shell. Of this root, five forms occur 13 times in the Qur’an: يَحُور yaḥūr once; يُحَاوِر yuḥāwir twice; تَحَاوُر taḥāwur once; حُور ḥūr four times and حَوَارِيُّون ḥawāriyyūn five times.
يَحُور yaḥūr [imperf. of v. حَارَ ḥāra, intrans.] to return, to go back (84:14) إِنَّهُ ظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ يَحُورَ and he thinks that he would not return [to his Lord].

يُحَاوِر yuḥāwir [imperf. of v. III حَاوَرَ ḥāwara, trans.] to debate with, to have a dialogue with (18:34) فَقَالَ لِصَاحِبِهِ وَهُوَ يُحَاوِرُهُ أَنَا أَكْثَرُ مِنْكَ مَالًا وَأَعَزُّ نَفَرًا so, he said to his friend, while disputing with him, ‘I am more than you in wealth, and mightier in respect of supporters.’

242 ح/و/ز

تَحَاوُر taḥāwur [v. n.] debating, engaging in a dialogue, disputing (58:1) قَدْ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ قَوْلَ الَّتِي تُجَادِلُكَ فِي زَوْجِهَا وَتَشْتَكِي إِلَى اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ يَسْمَعُ تَحَاوُرَكُمَا God has heard the words of her who disputes with you [Prophet] concerning her husband and complains to God and God hears your dispute.

حُور ḥūr [pl. of quasi-act. part. fem. حَوْرَاء ḥawrāʾ] pure, fair-skinned (also said to mean: having eyes marked by contrast between the deep black and the pure white in them), pleasant (44:54) كَذَٰلِكَ وَزَوَّجْنَاهُمْ بِحُورٍ عِينٍ so it will be, and We will wed them to wide-eyed houris.

اَلْحَوَارِيُّون ʾal-ḥawāriyyūn [pl. of n./quasi-act. part. حَوَارِيّ ḥawāriyy] the disciples of Christ (61:14) كَمَا قَالَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ لِلْحَوَارِيِّينَ مَنْ أَنْصَارِي إِلَى اللَّهِ قَالَ الْحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنْصَارُ اللَّهِ as Jesus, son of Mary, said to the disciples, ‘Who will come with me to help God?’, the disciples said, ‘We shall be God’s helpers.’
